replication_study.hypothesis: In countries that are classified as strong democracies (democ ≥ 9 in the replication dataset), larger political‐polarization in economic policy preferences – operationalised as the standard deviation of answers to the WVS "government should be responsible for providing jobs" item (sd_gov) – is associated with a smaller size of government, measured by government consumption as share of total consumption (gov_consumption). The expected sign of the coefficient of sd_gov is negative when controlling for the same set of covariates used in the original long specification.

data_plan.source_type: Cross–sectional country-level file built from World Values Survey micro-data merged with macro fiscal indicators
data_plan.wave_or_subset: WVS waves 5-7 (variable wave ∈ {5,6,7}); main run drops wave 7 observations, mirroring the logic in analyze.R
data_plan.sample_size: 31 country–wave observations (after list-wise deletion and optional wave-7 exclusion)
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Country-wave (one row per country for the reference year 2010 or the latest available WVS interview year within the wave)
data_plan.access_details: The compiled .csv is supplied in /app/data/original/17/input/replication_data; therefore no external download is required.

qualification.explanation: The file is assembled from the same international survey (WVS) and identical macro sources as the original study but uses later survey waves, giving an independent replication sample while preserving measurement comparability.
qualification.similarity_to_original:
- Same survey instrument: questions come from the WVS economic ideology battery (post_registration.json → “source”).
- Identical operationalisation of government size (gov_consumption) and many of the same covariates (region dummies, openness, demographics) – see columns in replication_data/data.csv and variable list in post_registration.json/"control_variables".
- Restriction to strong democracies (democ 9–10) exactly follows the focal claim (initial_details.txt).
qualification.deviation_from_original:
- Uses waves 5–7 (2005-2022) instead of waves 1995/2000 used originally (post_registration.json → “wave_or_subset”).
- Polarisation proxy is sd_gov (jobs-for-government question) instead of sd_private (private-ownership question named in initial_details.txt). Although still conceptually close, it provides a non-redundant test with different attitudinal content.
- Sample size is 31 vs 74, reflecting later-wave coverage and the democracy filter (replication_data/data.csv).

data_plan.notes: Country codes are in column scode; year is mostly 2010 because fiscal variables are anchored on that date. No missing values remain for key variables; thus listwise deletion suffices.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load replication_data/data.csv.
2. Optionally exclude observations with wave==7 (main specification) as coded in analyze.R.
3. Estimate ordinary least squares (OLS) of gov_consumption on sd_gov, mean_gov and the full set of controls from the original long specification.
4. Compute heteroskedasticity-robust (HC1) standard errors.
5. Record coefficient, standard error and p-value of sd_gov.
6. Repeat with wave 7 kept for robustness.
7. Compare sign, magnitude and significance against original coefficient −18.73 (initial_details.txt).
planned_method.models: OLS with HC1 heteroskedasticity-robust covariance matrix
planned_method.control_variables: africa, laam, asiae, col_uka, col_espa, col_otha, federal, oecd, log_gdp_per_capita, trade_share, age_15_64, age_65_plus
planned_method.tools_software: R ≥ 4.2, packages: lmtest, sandwich, readr/dplyr (base read.csv suffices)

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient of sd_gov in the long model

planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicated if coefficient on sd_gov is negative and p < 0.05 (two-sided). Directional consistency is required even if not significant for partial replication.

files.replication_data/analyze.R: Main analysis script. Reads /app/data/output/data.csv (produced earlier), shuffles variables if mode=="test" for CI checks, fits the long model with robust SE, exports results into /app/data/output/{mode}_{drop}.txt.
files.replication_data/data.csv: Cleaned country-level dataset with polarisation, outcome and all controls.

codebase.notes: No external build script is provided for creating data.csv; we rely on the pre-compiled file. Paths inside analyze.R must be edited so that input and output live in /app/data; otherwise script works as is.
